title: Prototype Insect-Proof Ventilating Window Installed in Tanzania
author: Andy Simmonds
body:
Prototype secure, insect proof ventilating window installed in local teachers' home in semi-rural Tanzania. Prototype researched and designed by ARC, developed from an earlier version used in the ecovillage by Hannah & Otis Sloan-Wood. Steel frame and mashrabiya security screen, (plasma cut) by Vogue engineering in Dar es Salaam. Painted. Insect mesh screen uses magnetic strip frame to secure to window frame. Low cill to allow whole body cooling (via breeze). Additional layers we will be experimenting with include, in-window Venetian blinds, for enhanced wind blown rain protection for exposed window positions, and additional privacy control, as well as air speed control.

Citizen scientist, ARC Tz champion, & school teacher, Dauda Suleiman and his wife (also a school teacher) will be providing user feedback: “We’re very happy with the installation of the prototype, it makes the house very attractive and adds to the house value. At this moment all my children do not want to sleep, they are still watching the window and asking a lot of questions to me.”

Thanks to Islamic Help’s Al-Mizan Children’s Ecovillage Engineers, Pemba and Azizi for the installation.
excerpt: Prototype secure, insect proof ventilating window installed in local teachers' home in semi-rural Tanzania — researched and designed by ARC, with a steel frame and mashrabiya security screen by Vogue Engineering in Dar es Salaam.
